programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan links a suspicious suicide to a dating agency, prompting Jack to go undercover to investigate - but he must take the deception further than anticipated to crack the case. Mystery drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The murder of a volleyball player reveals how much her team-mates disliked her, and a mysterious stalker only serves to complicate matters further for the sleuthing medic. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke, Victoria Rowell and Barry Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble's life is in jeopardy when he is the sole witness to the ball park kidnapping of a powerful newspaperman's daughter

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry Mason defends a man accused of killing a newspaper reporter who was searching for an escaped Nazi war criminal

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The crusty, crime-solving ranger tells a modern-day equivalent of Dickens' A Christmas Carol

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A man dressed in a superhero costume is found dead in an alley, and Mac, Stella and Dr Hawkes are taken aback to discover his everyday clothes in a nearby phone booth. Meanwhile, Danny and Lindsay investigate the murder of a promising NFL footballer. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Carmine Giovinazzo and Eddie Cahill

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Vance sticks rigidly to the rules as he investigates the death of a fellow agent, but Gibbs and Franks take matters into their own hands to catch the killers. The case also leads to major changes for the agency. Drama, starring Rocky Carroll, Mark Harmon and Muse Watson

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam, Kensi and Deeks cancel their Christmas plans and jet off to East Asia to investigate the death of a fellow NCIS special agent. They soon discover that a complicated murder has taken place and identify a pair of suspects. Meanwhile, back at headquarters, festive celebrations are getting under way as Nell and Eric make the most of the mistletoe lying around, and Hetty reveals that she is a real softie at heart. Drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble, the one-armed man and Gerard come face-to-face for the first time

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A former nightclub singer is shocked when her wealthy father-in-law offers her $50,000 to divorce his playboy son

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The family of a child who finds a gun suspected of being a murder weapon at a naval base comes under suspicion. The team sets out to discover who last used the firearm, with likely candidates including a petty criminal, a drug user, a Navy commander and a boy. Crime drama, starring Pauley Perrette and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Assassins try to kill Walker and the key witness he is escorting by blowing up their plane

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. She identifies the murderer as Seal-turned-assassin Marcellus Dane, who is suspected of working for Casey Stratton - a member of black ops unit Phantom Eight. Guest starring Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deeks' one-time LAPD informant Jenny Radler is kidnapped after uncovering dodgy dealings linking a lucrative car shipping outfit to a global terrorist ring. Facing a race against time, Kensi poses as a streetwise car thief to prove Jenny's findings, while the rest of the team members try to locate her before it's too late. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: CIA agent Trent Kort returns to track down dangerous criminal Jonathan Siravo and Gibbs is forced to put his personal feelings aside to assist in the investigation. The duo manage to get hold of Siravo's accountant, who agrees to reveal all he knows in exchange for full immunity, but the trail leads to an old man in a coma in what seems to be a case of stolen identity

programme.desc: Tracey's dream of having Darryl back home from prison finally comes true - but it may turn out to be a nightmare. Comedy, starring Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey and Darryl's efforts to make up for lost time are continuously thwarted by Sharon and Garth, while Dorien's marital strife seems set to cause further upheaval for Chigwell's ne'er-do-well clan. Comedy, with Pauline Quirke, Lesley Joseph and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A stabbing victim (guest star Christopher Colquhoun) accuses the husband of a woman he once had a brief affair with, but forensic evidence reveals his wounds are self-inflicted. Realising how unstable the man is, Smithy mounts a frantic search to stop him reaching the woman's daughter, and catches up with him at her school. As Gina evacuates the building, Smithy takes his life in his hands to disarm the ranting maniac

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Elizabeth wants to know why she was abused as a child, and struggles to accept her mother's decision to refuse treatment. The lies surrounding the plastics emergencies begin to unravel as Sahira discovers the use of illegal implants at the hospital, and Eddi tries to keep her distance from Josh

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Guppy gets stung while on a surfing trip, and the excursion goes from bad to worse when Greg is reminded of a painful memory-before a boating accident forces the staff into action. Harry vows to save the ED from closure by standing for MP and receives support from an unlikely source, while Nathan asks Selena an important question

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gina's last day with the Sun Hill watch reaches a climax with a tense stand-off as manic stalker Jake Quinn holds Smithy and two schoolgirls at gunpoint, refusing to talk to anyone except the woman he has been harassing. Callum and Rachel keep cool heads and reconnoitre the scene, while the Armed Response Unit is summoned to bring a swift resolution to the situation, but hearts sink when a shot rings out around the school

programme.desc: Joe is upset that Grant and Lorraine have split up. Tiffany tells Grant she loves him and that his future is with her and Courtney. Tony is beaten up

programme.desc: A woman becomes convinced she has contracted syphilis from her husband, who indignantly claims he has never had an affair - raising suspicions that she is the one who has been unfaithful. Starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James and Siegfried react very differently when the Veterinary Record publishes one of Calum's articles. Rural drama based upon the novels of James Herriot, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and John McGlynn

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Part one of two. Paul is called in to help with a rape case, but after examining the victim he is horrified to learn Kate is acting as the suspect's solicitor

programme.title: French Fields

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Geoffrey persuades Diana to see a doctor about her foot, but his suggestion of adopting a new diet doesn't go down well

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to take Daddy out for a picnic, but her plans soon backfire. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Nora dons a revealing outfit for a church pageant and is dared by Ivy to call Compo's bluff and present herself on his doorstep. Unfortunately, the shock proves too much for him and he is raced to hospital. Alas, he failed to make it through, presenting his friends with a dilemma on how best to commemorate him. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star in a special instalment of Roy Clarke's comedy, marking the departure of Bill Owen's character from the series

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: Lighthaven is stunned when a teenage girl appears to return from the dead, leaving the town to wonder where she has been for a year and who is buried in her place

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: A grieving Luther returns to work in the wake of his ex-wife's murder, and immediately finds himself plunged into a nightmarish case. A killer wearing a Punch mask has started to stalk the streets, and as the body count rises, it becomes clear he is determined to enter folklore. However, the detective's attentions are divided as he also tries to rescue an old friend's daughter from the dangerous world of prostitution. Crime thriller, starring Idris Elba and Ruth Wilson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A body is found, prompting two people to confess to murder. When tests show the corpse is more than 600 years old, the team must discover why the pair took responsibility for a crime they could not possibly have committed and if they are in fact the perpetrators of other killings. Alun Armstrong and James Bolam star

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Two long-dead bodies are found in a disused coal mine on the outskirts of Glasgow when it is reopened to serve as the central attraction for a museum. Investigations reveal the pair had been involved in violent encounters just before their deaths, and the search to discover their identities uncovers deeply buried resentments in the community. Detective drama, starring Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

Meanwhile, Sahira discovers Sunil bought cheap implants that turned out to be defective

programme.desc: While Richard contends with the idea of taking early retirement, Hyacinth telephones the prime minister asking if there is anything on the honours list for him. The hen-pecked husband's reservations about the future intensify when he discovers all the plans his spouse has in store for him. Comedy, starring Clive Swift and Patricia Routledge

programme.desc: As preparations get under way for Compo's funeral, Clegg is amazed to be given a letter written by the old rascal with strict instructions that it should only be opened in event of his death. Peter Sallis, Kathy Staff and Frank Thornton star

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley and Havers investigate when young lawyer Emily Proctor is found axed to death in Hyde Park. They are led to the chambers of a human rights barrister who was having an affair with her, and it isn't long before the victim's seedy double life involving Internet porn and dark family secrets emerges. Detective drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Samuel West

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens a 20-year-old case when a camera and film belonging to a photographic lab assistant murdered in 1987 are found by chance in a Soho pub toilet. New evidence leads the detectives into the world of modelling and to the door of a professional footballer. Meanwhile, Brian's erratic behaviour takes an alarming turn, and Gerry is less than happy when his daughter Emily (played by Dennis Waterman's real-life daughter Hannah) joins the team and looks to Sandra as a role model

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The search for an abducted student leads nowhere until a 'psychic adviser' to the missing girl's mother approaches the police, offering to help track her down. Burke grudgingly agrees to attend one of her shows and is stunned by the accuracy of information he is given about his father that no-one else could possibly know. Drama, starring Alex Norton and Blythe Duff

programme.desc: A homeless Kelsey resorts to desperate measures and agrees to work in a brothel, but is shocked to discover who her client is. Abs becomes emotionally involved with a patient suffering from cancer, while Nathan convinces Alice to lodge a formal complaint against Harry

With Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove

programme.desc: Charlie is appointed to defend an animal rights activist accused of murdering an eminent scientist - a case that will be heard before her father. programme.desc: Ross has his contact book stolen by a woman he brought home from the pub, and the next morning one of his informants is found murdered, with the word 'grass' daubed in red paint at the scene of the crime. programme.desc: There's an incident at the gym when Linda pushes herself too hard. Iqra supports Mila as she stands up for what she believes. programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Mischievous Basil pretends he has forgotten about his 15th wedding anniversary - but Sybil responds by walking out on him. With friends due to arrive for a celebratory party, the harassed hotelier is forced to take desperate steps to convince them all is well on the marital front. Guest starring Una Stubbs and Ken Campbell

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: The police have made an arrest and the fragile pact is at breaking point. As Anna, Nancy, Louie and Cat are interviewed, a frustrated Max decides to resort to more unorthodox methods to get to the truth

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: A farewell Christmas dinner for Jessie is interrupted by Tom, but any hopes of reconciliation are thrown off course by an encounter with a weed brownie

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Model Steve kept his relationship with ex Luise a secret - but was that so he could play the field? After seeing a suspicious video Tia wants to know what her ex Alex really got up to at Carnival? Now face to face will she get the truth? And Lewis is desperate to find out whether his ex Lara slept with one of his mates. Will she finally come clean and could they try again? Find out as we eavesdrop on these tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions.

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The contenders put their marketing hats on, as they are given the challenge to create and market a product, including producing an infomercial in front of a green screen

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, a mother and daughter from Swansea, and couples from Manchester and Surrey have their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

A nervous Max needs all the support she can get as the day of her operation arrives, and Ange and Josh risk being found out when Dom and Chloe catch them sneaking around together

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Nicky, a single mother to four teenage children in Royal Wootton Bassett, has a garden with tired turf, an ugly concrete path and no patio or decking to sit out on. What it does have, however, is Nicky's prized collection of gnomes. Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers pitch for the chance to come up with a fun, practical and social space that is easy to maintain - and which includes somewhere the gnomes can be proudly on display

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star

They must create make-up to complement different backdrops and it's a huge opportunity as their work will be viewed by millions of the DJ's fans. However, the worst two will face the penalty chairs for the creative brief, which this week is based on a guilty pleasure

programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a run-down mid-terrace in Dover bought by a plucky first-time developer. Martell Maxwell is in Dudley, West Midlands, viewing an old retail unit, while Dion Dublin is in Manchester looking round a real shell of a building

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers James and Mark help an 85-year-old man who is owed thousands by a roofing company who failed to pay the money the courts awarded

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A truck driver is diverted along a narrow road that cannot cope with the weight of his vehicle, with disastrous consequences. Paramedics treat an unresponsive baby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts as Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks help teams select items at the antiques fair at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, in the hope of making a profit at auction. The host also discovers an interesting find at the fair that is not to everyone's taste

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al helps a local councillor try to reconnect with his estranged, grieving son. Medical drama, starring Ian Midlane

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Shaun, Gina, John and Jane

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple search for a rural home in West Yorkshire with a budget of £500,000, which must have enough space to fit a beloved model railway. The presenter also visits one of the county's heritage fabric mills

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich brothers to win the pitch for an elaborate Chelsea Flower Show-inspired makeover in Gloucester. The vast plot of land wraps around the house and boasts a brook and beautiful views, but it's an uncoordinated and untidy mess that embarrasses home-owners Sam and Sean. They have £5,000 to put things right but it's an ambitious project. Whose design will win the day?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Natasha Raskin Sharp drive to the Scottish Borders, finding items including a tobacco barrel, an 18th-century teapot and a travel case with a secret compartment

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as more members of the public try to persuade the dealers to bid on their varied and unusual items. Shop owner Lindsay from Sunderland brings in a pair of optician's mirrors - one concave and the other convex - Lucinda has an item that has the dealers puzzled at first, until the owner reveals it is an antique ice shaver, and Sheffield man Dean hopes to make money from a vintage child's penny farthing

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalwork expert Dominic Chinea is called in to saved a beloved garden bench that is on its last legs. For owner Margaret, this is where she first met her husband in his mother's garden, and later was the seat her father took his tea breaks on. Steve Fletcher gets to work on an old calculator which is completed jammed up, Suzie Fletcher gives some much-needed TLC to an antique bridle and Tim Gunn tries to get a rusty 1930s toy scooter rolling again

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents as the contestants create garments inspired by the movies, beginning with a pattern challenge to make Baby's iconic dress from Dirty Dancing. For the transformation round, the judges take their inspiration from Julie Andrews in The Sound of Music, asking the sewers to turn old curtains into children's clothes, while the made-to-measure task sees them creating dazzling 1970s-influenced disco dresses, as worn by the Dreamgirls

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar tries to understand what drives people to trade and uncovers a link between an established financial brand with offices in the City of London and the case of Gurvin

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar gets closer to Gurvin and a meeting with a whistleblower raises questions about marketing and responsibility in the multi-billion-pound world of retail forex trading

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill visit Liverpool couple Tony and Nayna, who love being outdoors but find their new-build garden uninspiring and have run out of ideas to revitalise it. The pair hope that with Charlie and Lee's expertise and £4,500 budget they can secure a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, while Tony in particular dreams of a centre-piece water feature

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where two teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction on a budget of £300 each. Natasha monitors their progress and also takes a historic look at the semi-precious gemstone Blue John

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items include Chippendale tables with a hefty price tag, an original Punch and Judy set used to entertain children on the lawns of the hall, and an intriguing necklace linked to a secret society. There is also rare rock memorabilia, including a poster of Mick Jagger by Andy Warhol

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The first of the North West region heats sees Tom Parker, Paul Askew and Ellis Barrie prepare their starters for the banquet marking 140 years of the Wimbledon tennis championships. The dishes include a salad made up of 10 complex elements, and a cucumber and oyster creation, with a Great British Menu veteran judging their efforts

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: North West chefs Paul Askew, Ellis Barrie and Tom Parker produce fish-based dishes, with Ellis aiming to create the flavour of a summer barbecue without actually using one. Tom serves oysters and langoustines with a risky champagne sauce that combines both traditional British and Asian flavours, while Paul cooks turbot and oysters

programme.title: Guys and Dolls (1955)
programme.desc: A gambler accepts a bet that he cannot win the heart of a strait-laced Salvation Army member. Meanwhile, his friend has to find a secret location for an illegal dice game he is organising - while also contending with the police, a mobster and a girlfriend desperate to drag him to the altar. Musical, starring Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando and Jean Simmons. Songs include Luck Be a Lady Tonight and Sit Down You're Rocking the Boat

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Looking at the Hollywood musical, with interviews from stars such as Fred Astaire and Julie Andrews, as well as discussion of films like Singin' in the Rain and The Sound of Music

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher are in Ushaw in Co Durham, seeking a selection of objects to take to auction. Catherine falls in love with a model of Muffin the Mule and David is enchanted by a limited edition book by author JK Rowling. Presenter Paul Martin learns what life was like for the students who studied at Ushaw when it was open as a Catholic college

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Four famous faces go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week, with comedians Nish Kumar and Al Murray, Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo and broadcaster Anneka Rice the first contestants. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the weekly scores are tallied resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday, when the victor chooses between a House of Games dartboard and a branded dressing gown

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Britain of the 1930s as he sets out on another adventure. Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation that led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the government for work. The first leg of his journey ends Spennymoor, Co Durham, where he meets the son of a miner who became one of the most famous 20th-century artists of the region

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson reveals a secret passion when she prepares her dark and deviant liquorice pudding, then extols the virtues of fregola - the Sardinian sun-dried and toasted couscous - with tomato and clams. Her dinner-party guests are served butterflied leg of lamb and baby aubergines with oregano and roasted red onion, followed by meringue gelato cake with sharp raspberries and dark chocolate sauce for dessert

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by Don Warrington, Zoe Lyons, Hugh Dennis and Giovanna Fletcher, who discuss Agent Running in the Field by John le Carré and Another Life by Jodie Chapman, and also share their own favourite books of all time

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Will Foster and Lynsey Elliott compete to remodel a home in Gosport, Hampshire, that holds 20 years of family memories and a lifetime of antiques and heirlooms for soon-to-be grandparents Lisa and Judy. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: It's time for the Year 6 school trip, and Julia, Liz and Meg find themselves on the coach journey from Hell with humourless school secretary Mrs Lamb and clueless teaching assistant Ms Vaughn. It all kicks off when a boy banned for bad behaviour is allowed on the bus, along with his mother Ashley, and Liz decides to play the hero. Meanwhile, Kevin takes his driving test and Amanda puts her lifestyle brand Hygge Tygge online

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Actor James is waiting for his big moment on ITV's new crime drama in a static caravan that is owned by a strange family, who clearly have something to hide. Darkly comic drama, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Adrian Dunbar and Pauline McLynn

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor and comedian David Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and broadcaster the Rev Richard Coles joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Film Stars Don't Die in Liverpool (2017)
programme.desc: Real life proves to be just as dramatic off-screen as it does on it for aging Hollywood superstar Gloria Grahame and her much younger lover, Peter Turner. As their mismatched romance waxes and wanes over time, events conspire to keep them in each other's lives even when it proves to be difficult and demanding. Romantic drama, starring Jamie Bell and Annette Bening

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Special episode devoted to community gardens and planting wildflowers, exploring their benefits both to the well-being of the people who make use of them and to the environment, with positive influences on climate change and bee populations. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor help Bristol locals to create a community plot, and Charlotte Smith visits a blooming project in Glasgow. Sean Fletcher reports on a field to fork project involving children in Northern Ireland, and Helen Skelton meets a botanist working to preserve endangered wildflowers

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories, with the odds stacked against children from the West Indies. Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented time, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines. University Hospital Coventry's gynaecological oncology department deals with rising referrals, a growing backlog and complex cases as the hospital's theatre capacity attempts to return to normal

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers help Adam and Frederique in Bracknell to turn their front garden into an upcycled, child-friendly eco paradise with vegetable beds for £3,000

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Leominster, Herefordshire, and visits an antique doll restorer. Experts Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri help out today's teams

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don adds flowers to act as pollinators and creates an alpine trough. Adam Frost helps comedian Susan Calman with her garden, and Nick Bailey reveals a simple way to attract wildlife

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Hull FC and Catalans Dragons v Wigan Warriors

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents an appeal on behalf of Changing Faces, who offer practical and emotional support to anyone in the UK living with a scar, mark or condition

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin teaches two would-be antiques dealers the tricks of the trade, pitching Piers Taberham from Southampton against Krystal Allen from Belfast, before an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North West chefs go all out to impress with their main courses, as they compete for a chance to cook at the Taste of Summer banquet. Paul Askew creates a dish based on his mother's favourite food, Ellis Barrie offers up an imaginative course inspired by former Wimbledon champion Fred Perry, and Tom Parker presents his take on a traditional summer barbecue

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Ellis Barrie, Tom Parker and Paul Askew continue the battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to tomorrow's North West final

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Jeff Turner spends a year in northern Canada filming the life of a wolf pack that featured in the David Attenborough series Frozen Planet. The cameraman learns how the animals survive in the harsh wilderness and discovers whether the pack leader can pass its hunting skills on to a new generation of cubs

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of experts detail the lives of marine animals visiting and thriving in UK waters each summer, beginning by finding out where on the coast it is possible to see dolphins and whales. With contributions by wildlife cameraman Richard Taylor-Jones and BBC Springwatch Extra presenter Lindsey Chapman

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston upon Hull, Caroline Hawley uncovers a remarkable collection of suffragette postcards and David Harper is delighted by some Hull Daily Mail newspaper printing blocks, saved from the tip. Presented by Paul Martin

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nish Kumar, Clara Amfo, Anneka Rice and Al Murray test their general knowledge skills in more trivia-based games

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands, heading to Kielder Forest to discover what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest manmade woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business, and also goes to the movies, sitting in the front stalls at the Dominion cinema in Morningside

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Red Dwarf co-stars Craig Charles and Robert Llewellyn travel around the West Midlands in search of antiques, assisted by experts Izzie Balmer and Tim Medhurst. Robert is smitten with a 200-year-old love letter, while Craig splurges on a multitude of quirky curios

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan report live from Norfolk, and Iolo Williams heads up into the Scottish Highlands to spot some flamboyant avian show-offs. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the third lockdown begins to lift in the middle of April 2021, emergency cases inevitably begin to rise, which puts an incredible amount of pressure of bed availability and staff across the country. At Coventry's University Hospital, Critical Care is at full capacity, and two neurosurgeons must find a solution to treat both of their patients with only one bed available

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Gyles Brandreth, Victoria Coren Mitchell and Jimmy Carr join series regular Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme of 'not nearly' in an extended edition of the comedy panel show. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part one of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first Northern Ireland parliament. Fearful for their lives, they had come to a city scarred by bitter sectarian violence. One hundred years on, this is the story of the dramatic events that led to the partition of Ireland - one that continues to reverberate to the present day

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation uncovering new evidence of A.I. being used by police in China to recognise the emotions of detainees, in order to help determine guilt or innocence. China has vowed to become the world's A.I. superpower by 2030, sparking a new arms race with America, and both countries are pouring billions into cutting-edge military tech, including autonomous weapons. A.I. could usher in a golden age, but without urgent regulation, experts warn we could lose control of artificial intelligence, a prospect, they say, that should scare us all

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop & Power
programme.desc: Little Mix star Leigh-Anne Pinnock recounts her experiences of racism and colourism as a black woman in the UK, as well as looking at the wider race issues the country is facing. The girl band member compares experiences with Alexandra Burke, Nao, RAYE and Keisha Buchanan from the Sugababes, who each reveal their own shocking experiences within the music industry because of the colour of their skin. Leigh-Anne finds the more she learns about systemic racism, the more she feels she must do something

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Harsimrat and Jagjit celebrate the arrival of their first-born baby at home, while 15-year-old Ansh learns about the sacred meaning of his turban. Newlyweds Hari and Kiran continue their devotion to their guru through their marriage and in their daily lives, and Hardeep scatters his father's ashes over flowing water to unite him with God

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers take on the job of redesigning an unusually shaped garden in Droitwich Spa, whose owners want different areas for entertaining and relaxing

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of early retirees find a new home in Wiltshire or Somerset - but it must be a period property with high ceilings and at least four bedrooms

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian find out about mental health in men and boys, and learn the importance of wild gardens and try out a running app. With voiceover

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham reviews the success of Beechgrove's box-hedging alternatives, while he and Mairi Rattray also share topiary tips. Plus, catching up with the Seil Community Garden, which Beechgrove helped create in 2009.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts the game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts the antiques-buying challenge from the Midlands, where Emma Wilson from Teesside and David Broughton from Sussex go head to head going to auction and buy collectable items which they hope to sell on to maximize their profits

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs compete for a place in the national finals, preparing their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Greg Rusedski. Greg, a former British number one tennis player, understands the high standards expected at his sport's Grand Slam contests, in this Wimbledon-themed series

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The first of the North East heats sees returning champion Tommy Banks and newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington prepare their starters. Tommy draws inspiration from the traditional celebration of midsummer, Danny creates a dish using 22 vegetables, while Josh's centres on a savoury strawberry veloute

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan report live from Norfolk, and Iolo Williams heads up into the Scottish Highlands to spot some flamboyant avian show-offs. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Big Blue UK
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and his team find out where sharks can be spotted in waters surrounding the UK, while Lindsay Chapman looks for shark egg cases washed up on our shore. Can she also find live shark embryos in amongst Devon's seaweed?

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston upon Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper hunt for objects to take to auction. Caroline is entertained by the story behind some Laurel and Hardy autographs and David is gripped by a collection of postcards between young lovers from the Edwardian era. Presenter Paul Martin talks to playwright James Graham about the legacy of Hull's year as the UK's City of Culture

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nish Kumar, Clara Amfo, Anneka Rice and Al Murray continue to test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the Westerglen Transmitting Station in Falkirk, before visiting Gleneagles and Berry Town on route to his final stop on this leg, the city of Dundee

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day one of the First Test in the two-match series, held at Lord's. The sides last met in this format on English soil in 2015, when the hosts recorded a 124-run victory here, before the Black Caps ensured the series ended in a draw by prevailing by 199 runs at Headingley

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Insect fan Penny Metal introduces viewers to the tiny residents in a London park, and Iolo Williams checks out some alien species on the forest floor at Alladale Wilderness Reserve. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at spring from a swift's point of view at WWT Castle Espie

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This series follows the engineers, technicians, and the behind-the-scenes staff who are under pressure to keep the project on track, including building the mammoth foundations for the two nuclear reactors, and excavating cooling water tunnels out under the Bristol Channel

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Rob Beckett plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by rising comedy stars Jen Brister and Darren Harriott

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the third lockdown begins to lift in the middle of April 2021, emergency cases inevitably begin to rise, which puts an incredible amount of pressure of bed availability and staff across the country. At Coventry's University Hospital, Critical Care is at full capacity, and two neurosurgeons must find a solution to treat both of their patients with only one bed available

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian find out about mental health in men and boys, and learn the importance of wild gardens and try out a running app. With voiceover

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Plant-able children's books are the offering from two friends hoping to gain a Dragon to grow their brand, and a pair of ex-bankers pitch their ambitious at-home private chef service. A Wirral-based entrepreneur pitches his range of luxury home fragrance products, while a husband-and-wife duo are keen to broaden out their scent-detection courses for dogs. Whether multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies will smell any potential profit remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard and guests discuss how we remember - not just as individuals, but as a society and a nation. She is joined by architect David Adjaye, writer Elif Shafak, artists Edmund de Waal and Cornelia Parker, poet Lemn Sissay, and actor Jane Horrocks

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to leafy Hatch End in North West London, where he meets the great-nephew of the famous illustrator William Heath Robinson. Michael continues his journey through Slough and Stoke Poges before reaching Windsor, where he surveys the great walls of the castle, chief residence of the British monarch and the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones sets about restoring the 200-year-old Trehilyn Farm in north Pembrokeshire. In this first edition, the comedian plans the project, in which he will replace the roof tiles with individually carved Welsh slates, and apply a white lime render to the stone walls

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints mountains and a waterfall enclosed by evergreens and mossy cliffs

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers use their smartphones to capture fast-moving action in a boxing arena, and are set further challenges by artist Maryam Wahid and celebrity stylist Cheryl Konteh

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy
programme.desc: George Smiley has talked to Jim Prideaux about his interrogation by the Russians - but is even more interested to hear what happened when he returned to England. Penultimate part of the classic 1970s espionage drama, based on the novel by John le Carre, starring Alec Guinness, Ian Bannen and Joss Ackland

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy
programme.desc: Smiley devises a plan he hopes will see the trap close on Karla's mole, who will be used as a trade against several English spies captured by the KGB. Alec Guinness stars as masterspy George Smiley in the conclusion of the classic 1970s espionage drama

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: An interview from 2008 in which the best-selling author discusses his life and work, including the novels Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy and The Spy Who Came in from the Cold. Famous for writing espionage thrillers, Le Carre started as a real-life spy working for MI6, and wrote the first of his hugely popular books while in the secret service

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: As the school year begins, a new mum at the gates attracts the attention of Julia, Liz and Kevin, with Amanda telling them she is a high-flying businesswoman with five kids and a sexy husband. Julia is struggling to keep her head above water at work when she is offered the choice of promotion or redundancy. Comedy about the trials and traumas of motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Having resigned from her job in PR, Julia tries to adapt to life as a freelancer and reluctantly agrees to help Amanda launch her new shop - even though she believes it is nothing but a vanity project. The opening is a success, but a careless online post threatens to destroy her good work. Meanwhile, Liz rails against the gentrification of the area and the loss of her beloved kebab shop, while Kevin reveals he has written an unusual children's book, which he wants Amanda to sell in her shop

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia finds that working from home is impossible due to all the tasks she has to do for her family. Meg tells her this is her 'Mother's Load' - all the things that no-one else will do, but need to be done to keep the home running. Julia removes herself to the cafe to work, but even there finds it's not that easy to get motivated. A possible solution presents itself when she shares a table with a fellow freelancer, but is Julia taking advantage of him? Meanwhile, much to her surprise, Liz finds herself bored to tears when her youngest starts nursery and she is alone during the day, and Kevin has problems when he takes on responsibility for a school fund-raising project

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Kevin reluctantly hosts a last-minute Halloween party, while Julia takes the kids trick-or-treating in an attempt to meet a celebrity who has just moved into the neighbourhood. Liz tags along as she has been stood up by a date and Anne is in attendance trying to make sure that the kids are safe - but by the time they reach Kevin's house one of the children is missing

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Everyone is delighted when Kevin books a cottage for them to spend half term in the countryside with all their children. But the house is not as nice as the pictures online suggested, and it is not long before tempers begin to fray. Anna Maxwell Martin, Diane Morgan and Paul Ready star

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia struggles to abide by the rules and clashes with Mrs Lamb as she attends her first-ever school sports day, where apparently it's no longer acceptable to cheer for individual children and spectators can only offer generic support like 'good job'. Over at the Kidiverse soft-play centre, Kevin is devastated that an important work presentation means he will miss the dads' race. Liz worries that her new boyfriend is blanking her, and Anne discovers something that leads to a nasty surprise for Amanda

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones sets about restoring the 200-year-old Trehilyn Farm in north Pembrokeshire. In this first edition, the comedian plans the project, in which he will replace the roof tiles with individually carved Welsh slates, and apply a white lime render to the stone walls

programme.title: A Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Nature intervenes as comedian Griff Rhys Jones continues in his attempts to restore his 200-year-old farmhouse in Pembrokeshire

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he paints a riverside cabin on an oval canvas, bursting with glorious foliage

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Plans to manufacture a controversial chemical at a factory on Merseyside cause disagreement between Jim Hacker and Sir Humphrey. Brenda Blethyn guest stars

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere moves his multinational grocery business to the manor and clashes with Audrey over the removal of a fireplace that he considers ugly and she believes is intrinsic to the property. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The satirist examines Britain during the Industrial Revolution and Victorian era, when writers, artists and politicians tried to make sense of the changing world by seeking inspiration from the Middle Ages. Novelist Walter Scott's tales of gallant heroes and knights in armour in his books Waverley and Ivanhoe brought him huge fame and success, while dissatisfied workers formed an ancient-style band of brothers to protest against industrialisation, calling themselves the Luddites. The Houses of Parliament were rebuilt in a medieval gothic style and Prime Minister Benjamin Disraeli led a government that improved living and working conditions for millions by harking back to the values of the past

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Steven Johnson and David Olusoga consider how data is the first line of defence against a new pandemic, before a vaccine or an effective treatment can come into play

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Dr Kevin Fong explores the boom in apps and gadgets designed to help people monitor their health around the clock. He meets some of the pioneers of this revolution and visits a man who self-diagnosed a life-threatening disease from his own data, without going to the doctor

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley's three-part documentary exploring the history of medicine concludes as he tells the story of how scientists went from finding antidotes to poisons to applying otherwise dangerous toxins as a cure. He reveals why he believes, of all the natural remedies featured in his scientific tour, the tales of treatments derived from deadly substances are perhaps the most extraordinary he encountered

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford investigates the story of the two brilliant but controversial Hunter brothers, who transformed both medicine and art in 18th-century Britain. Their belief that students could only learn anatomy by carrying out dissections created an unprecedented demand for dead bodies - as well as a market for the growing trade of body-snatching from graveyards

programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he paints a riverside cabin on an oval canvas, bursting with glorious foliage

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Racing champion Martin, who is a member of Britain's oldest flying club The Royal Aero Club, prepares to compete for the Schneider Trophy, in which 14 pilots will fly around outlying islands in Alderney in the Channel Islands. Elsewhere, a cargo pilot contends with a tight schedule, and must cross the Irish sea even during bad weather conditions, while the team at DHL's warehouse processes a hundred and fifty thousand parcels each night. Plus, at NATS, bad weather grounds planes and dangerous 'wind sheer' causes pilots to abort landings

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The documentary investigates the deadly impact of an incendiary bomb that set fire to St Peter's Church in the historic heart of Bristol on the night of Sunday November 24, 1940. Almost 80 years later, Geoffrey Serle, who had been attending church with his father when the raid began, recounts their desperate attempt to find shelter amongst the chaos. The film also uses accounts from the time, recalling how Bristol's firefighters simply could not cope

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary following workers at Rolls-Royce's Derby factory as they construct the company's Trent 1000 jet engine in preparation for its maiden flight attached to a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. The film explores the technology involved in its construction, and charts the ups and downs of working on the assembly line. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Ashwika is passionate about protecting India’s only ape: the hoolock gibbon. Spending most of their lives in the canopy and rarely coming to ground means their populations are hugely threatened by deforestation, with their population decreasing by 90 per cent in just 30 years. Four years ago, Ashwika travelled to Assam and met a gibbon that touched her heart, and from that moment on she has wanted to protect them. On this mission she is going to a village where the people live peacefully alongside the gibbons and have done for many generations, and learns about why they care about them so much. Along the wa,y she witnesses habitat loss with her own ‘eye in the sky’. And she meets one of India’s top gibbon scientists, who is doing everything he can to save the species with the help of local villagers.

programme.title: Odd Squad

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: When Ms O's important briefcase is stolen by a shape shifter, Olive and Otto set out to retrieve it

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The rodent's film star status is threatened by a toilet uprising

programme.title: Ricky Wilson's Art Jam
programme.desc: TV presenter and DJ Kem Cetinay visits the Art Jam studio to show Ricky three items that mean the world to him - his first pair of hairdressing scissors, his dog Rocky’s collar and his grandfather’s shepherd whistle. While chatting to Ricky, Kem shares amazing stories about how learning to be a hairdresser boosted his confidence, how his dog Rocky has helped improve his mental health, his incredible family history and the fun he has as a TV presenter.

programme.title: Don't Blame Me, Blame My Brain
programme.desc: Ken's brain is searching for an answer to the question of whether we can talk with dogs. Leila knows just where to look, while Ken has help from Storm Huntley with her dog Boo.

programme.title: We Bare Bears
programme.desc: Charlie befriends a forest snake while trying to find food for the bears

programme.title: Summer Camp Island

programme.title: Mighty Mustangs

programme.title: Our Black History Heroes

programme.title: Mimi and Co
programme.desc: Mimi's friend Cynthia takes her to a dog groomer to overcome her fear of dogs, while Mimi masters the art of posing with internet hit cat Nala. We also meet Fred, who has over 60 pets!

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Erin Ranney is a wildlife camera operator and adventurer. She splits her time between Alaska and the Falklands and loves filming the planet’s most incredible wildlife. Erin’s mission is to film rockhopper penguins on the Falkland Islands and find out why these charismatic creatures are facing serious threats to their survival. She investigates why the rockhopper population has fallen by 80 per cent since the 1930s. Climate change is the biggest threat to the rockhoppers, causing violent storms that could see them penguins face extinction. Erin also finds out how planting tussac grass can combat climate change and discovers that what we do in our own countries can affect these penguins thousands of miles away. She talks about how we can all make a difference towards helping the planet, just by taking small steps

programme.title: The Next Step

programme.title: The Demon Headmaster

Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate shopping-themed toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to discover with their baby! Some viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring shopping too. The fun children’s song Shopping Day features, as does a story about Baby Bear at the shops. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join nine-month-old Bonnie and Harry as they discover the world around them and become more expressive. Both children love playing with their families - laughing, smiling, clapping, playing peek-a-boo and making noise. Bonnie gets excited when her dad suggests a day out to the beach! When they get there, Bonnie is concentrating on making sandcastles before splashing her feet in the water, laughing and loving the sea. Harry and his family go on a bug hunt at a nature reserve, but what will they find? While the family enjoys a relaxing picnic, Harry tries a banana for the very first time. Bonnie is happily crawling along the beach finding seashells before mum lays out a picnic with yummy hummus and pitta bread. Before leaving, there is much laughter as they decide to try and completely cover dad with sand. After their picnic, Harry’s decides to join his brother in having his photo taken, and it’s big smiles all around!

programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Araiya, Oakley, Tommy, Sullivan, Samaa and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel has a book inside his bag. All the parents and babies have books with different words and pictures too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate book inside a fabric bag or pillowcase to explore with their baby. Some viewers at home show how they’ve been reading books and sharing stories too. The nursery rhyme This Is the Way We Read a Book features, as does a story about Baby Bear’s favourite book. The Together Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

Rudi and Everley begin their day eating breakfast with their families. Everley and her family are in the garden, looking at each other’s teeth in a mirror and counting how many they have. Rudi then plays with a teething mitten to soothe his teeth, before having fresh fruit. Everley visits her family dentist, where the dentist checks her teeth and talks to mum about the importance of teething. Once home, Everley’s mum gives Everley her very own toothbrush! Mum brushes her own teeth and shows Everley how to brush her teeth. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich brothers to win the pitch for an elaborate Chelsea Flower Show-inspired makeover in Gloucester. The vast plot of land wraps around the house and boasts a brook and beautiful views, but it's an uncoordinated and untidy mess that embarrasses home-owners Sam and Sean. They have £5,000 to put things right but it's an ambitious project. Whose design will win the day?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Natasha Raskin Sharp drive to the Scottish Borders, finding items including a tobacco barrel, an 18th-century teapot and a travel case with a secret compartment

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as more members of the public try to persuade the dealers to bid on their varied and unusual items. Shop owner Lindsay from Sunderland brings in a pair of optician's mirrors - one concave and the other convex - Lucinda has an item that has the dealers puzzled at first, until the owner reveals it is an antique ice shaver, and Sheffield man Dean hopes to make money from a vintage child's penny farthing

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalwork expert Dominic Chinea is called in to saved a beloved garden bench that is on its last legs. For owner Margaret, this is where she first met her husband in his mother's garden, and later was the seat her father took his tea breaks on. Steve Fletcher gets to work on an old calculator which is completed jammed up, Suzie Fletcher gives some much-needed TLC to an antique bridle and Tim Gunn tries to get a rusty 1930s toy scooter rolling again

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents as the contestants create garments inspired by the movies, beginning with a pattern challenge to make Baby's iconic dress from Dirty Dancing. For the transformation round, the judges take their inspiration from Julie Andrews in The Sound of Music, asking the sewers to turn old curtains into children's clothes, while the made-to-measure task sees them creating dazzling 1970s-influenced disco dresses, as worn by the Dreamgirls

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar tries to understand what drives people to trade and uncovers a link between an established financial brand with offices in the City of London and the case of Gurvin

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar gets closer to Gurvin and a meeting with a whistleblower raises questions about marketing and responsibility in the multi-billion-pound world of retail forex trading

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.desc: Emma reacts strangely to some good news, Norma's patience begins to wear thin and Sheriff Romero finally decides where his loyalties lie

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: The judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Michael Angus and Kate Spiers head to the Highlands to consider a new build in Aviemore inspired by Charles Rennie Mackintosh and two historic homes in Inverness and the Beauly Firth

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: There's an incident at the gym when Linda pushes herself too hard. Iqra supports Mila as she stands up for what she believes. Kheerat asks Sharon to keep their relationship quiet in a bid to keep on Ben's good side, and Mo announces she has been invited on a cruise with Fat Elvis - but is not going because she is worried about Jean

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Mischievous Basil pretends he has forgotten about his 15th wedding anniversary - but Sybil responds by walking out on him. With friends due to arrive for a celebratory party, the harassed hotelier is forced to take desperate steps to convince them all is well on the marital front. Guest starring Una Stubbs and Ken Campbell

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: The police have made an arrest and the fragile pact is at breaking point. As Anna, Nancy, Louie and Cat are interviewed, a frustrated Max decides to resort to more unorthodox methods to get to the truth

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: A farewell Christmas dinner for Jessie is interrupted by Tom, but any hopes of reconciliation are thrown off course by an encounter with a weed brownie

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Model Steve kept his relationship with ex Luise a secret - but was that so he could play the field? After seeing a suspicious video Tia wants to know what her ex Alex really got up to at Carnival? Now face to face will she get the truth? And Lewis is desperate to find out whether his ex Lara slept with one of his mates. Will she finally come clean and could they try again? Find out as we eavesdrop on these tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions.

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The contenders put their marketing hats on, as they are given the challenge to create and market a product, including producing an infomercial in front of a green screen

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, a mother and daughter from Swansea, and couples from Manchester and Surrey have their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the ambulance on its way, Sharon is shocked when Linda confides in her the truth about her pregnancy - especially when she reveals Mick is not the father. Jean manages to convince Mo to go on the cruise by lying about her recent behaviour, and the Slaters throw her a going-away party. Kheerat asks Ben for help protecting Jags in prison

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian convinces Andrei to return to hospital to have the surgery he needs, so Fletch warns Lucky to call the social services. A nervous Max needs all the support she can get as the day of her operation arrives, and Ange and Josh risk being found out when Dom and Chloe catch them sneaking around together

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Nicky, a single mother to four teenage children in Royal Wootton Bassett, has a garden with tired turf, an ugly concrete path and no patio or decking to sit out on. What it does have, however, is Nicky's prized collection of gnomes. Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers pitch for the chance to come up with a fun, practical and social space that is easy to maintain - and which includes somewhere the gnomes can be proudly on display

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: Nancy makes an important decision about her future, while Louie is stunned when Arwel's lawyer presents her with an opportunity to build a new family legacy. DS Holland is confident the women were involved in the killing and decides to make her move. Finally, the police net tightens, and the truth about Jack's murder is revealed - and the lives of the four friends are changed for ever

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: Maya Jama presents the semi-final, which sees the make-up artists create looks for the backing dancers in DJ Jonas Blue's new music video - directed by world-renowned photographer and guest judge Rankin. They must create make-up to complement different backdrops and it's a huge opportunity as their work will be viewed by millions of the DJ's fans. However, the worst two will face the penalty chairs for the creative brief, which this week is based on a guilty pleasure

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: The entrepreneurs prepare to capitalise on Valentine's Day, with Bridgend-based Scott ordering in 1,500 hand-decorated love-themed doughnuts. programme.desc: With the drive for Scottish independence gathering pace, Jim Fitzpatrick asks voters what the latest election results mean for the future of the UK, and how an independent Scotland might affect Northern Ireland's place in the Union

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: Maya Jama presents the semi-final, which sees the make-up artists create looks for the backing dancers in DJ Jonas Blue's new music video - directed by world-renowned photographer and guest judge Rankin. They must create make-up to complement different backdrops and it's a huge opportunity as their work will be viewed by millions of the DJ's fans. However, the worst two will face the penalty chairs for the creative brief, which this week is based on a guilty pleasure

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: The entrepreneurs prepare to capitalise on Valentine's Day, with Bridgend-based Scott ordering in 1,500 hand-decorated love-themed doughnuts. At Mallows Beauty, Laura and Ronnie are flat out trying to meet demand on their Valentine's Day gift box, while in Cardiff, Caribbean take-away owner Charlotte comes up with a new idea - romantic meals for two in portable pop-up igloos

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman continues to unravel while spending the day with one of the motel's guests, and Norma struggles to hide the pair's secrets as her concern for her son's condition grows

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a run-down mid-terrace in Dover bought by a plucky first-time developer. Martell Maxwell is in Dudley, West Midlands, viewing an old retail unit, while Dion Dublin is in Manchester looking round a real shell of a building

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers James and Mark help an 85-year-old man who is owed thousands by a roofing company who failed to pay the money the courts awarded

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A truck driver is diverted along a narrow road that cannot cope with the weight of his vehicle, with disastrous consequences. Paramedics treat an unresponsive baby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts as Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks help teams select items at the antiques fair at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, in the hope of making a profit at auction. The host also discovers an interesting find at the fair that is not to everyone's taste

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al helps a local councillor try to reconnect with his estranged, grieving son. Medical drama, starring Ian Midlane

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Shaun, Gina, John and Jane

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple search for a rural home in West Yorkshire with a budget of £500,000, which must have enough space to fit a beloved model railway. The presenter also visits one of the county's heritage fabric mills

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock takes on the Rich brothers to win the pitch for an elaborate Chelsea Flower Show-inspired makeover in Gloucester. The vast plot of land wraps around the house and boasts a brook and beautiful views, but it's an uncoordinated and untidy mess that embarrasses home-owners Sam and Sean. They have £5,000 to put things right but it's an ambitious project. Whose design will win the day?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Natasha Raskin Sharp drive to the Scottish Borders, finding items including a tobacco barrel, an 18th-century teapot and a travel case with a secret compartment

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as more members of the public try to persuade the dealers to bid on their varied and unusual items. Shop owner Lindsay from Sunderland brings in a pair of optician's mirrors - one concave and the other convex - Lucinda has an item that has the dealers puzzled at first, until the owner reveals it is an antique ice shaver, and Sheffield man Dean hopes to make money from a vintage child's penny farthing

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalwork expert Dominic Chinea is called in to saved a beloved garden bench that is on its last legs. For owner Margaret, this is where she first met her husband in his mother's garden, and later was the seat her father took his tea breaks on. Steve Fletcher gets to work on an old calculator which is completed jammed up, Suzie Fletcher gives some much-needed TLC to an antique bridle and Tim Gunn tries to get a rusty 1930s toy scooter rolling again

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents as the contestants create garments inspired by the movies, beginning with a pattern challenge to make Baby's iconic dress from Dirty Dancing. For the transformation round, the judges take their inspiration from Julie Andrews in The Sound of Music, asking the sewers to turn old curtains into children's clothes, while the made-to-measure task sees them creating dazzling 1970s-influenced disco dresses, as worn by the Dreamgirls

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and tweet

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar tries to understand what drives people to trade and uncovers a link between an established financial brand with offices in the City of London and the case of Gurvin

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar gets closer to Gurvin and a meeting with a whistleblower raises questions about marketing and responsibility in the multi-billion-pound world of retail forex trading

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Emma reacts strangely to some good news, Norma's patience begins to wear thin and Sheriff Romero finally decides where his loyalties lie

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: he debate in the House of Commons second chamber, Westminster Hall, marking World Press Freedom Day 2021, from Thursday 27 May.

programme.desc: The debate in the House of Commons second chamber, Westminster Hall, on an e-petition on child food poverty, from Monday 24 May.

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Under cover of Covid, the Amazon rainforest is under attack. Deforestation is at levels not seen for more than a decade. Brazilian President, Jair Bolsonaro, talks of opening up the forest to development while the environmental police is under attack from loggers. Our World has obtained a recording of the Environment minister talking about using the cover of Covid to 'change all the rules' in the Amazon. For Our World, Justin Rowlatt, is on a mission to find out how a tribe he visited a decade ago is faring the face of this assault
