programme.desc: Matilda's power makes her and Charlie end up inside the video game they are playing. They become the game characters, but all the actions they perform have an impact on real life

programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to turn a tower of jelly into art and make a magnet-powered motor. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald

programme.title: Kerry is Kirsty
programme.desc: Starting at the foot of Stac Polly, sisters Kerry and Kirsty MacPhee cycle round Coigeach with stunning views of the Summer Isles as a reward for their struggle against high winds. They meet up with Seonaidh Charity, who first got to know his chosen route as he'd often travel the road to visit his now fiancee

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A look at some of the more unusual war vehicles, featuring a one-man tank and a bicycle battalion

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Sgeul Seolaidh
programme.desc: The story of the significant role played by Gaels in the British Merchant Navy. Sailors from the Highlands and Islands reminisce, providing a unique insight into what it was like to sail the deep seas

programme.title: Thar na Loidhne/Crossing the Line
programme.desc: Stornoway Ladies prepare for a big game against the Orkney Dragons

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Personal stories about epilepsy, featuring contributions from some of the 54,000 people in Scotland with the condition, as well as their families and carers. Plus, a look at some of the different types of seizures and how the right kind of treatment can make a significant difference

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis presents the music series, offering musicians an opportunity to perform directly from their homes to viewing fans

programme.title: Cinema Gadelica
programme.desc: How film-makers have been drawn to the Highlands and Islands to shoot amid the spectacular scenery of the Gaidhealtachd. Discover James Bond’s Glencoe birthplace, as revealed in Skyfall. Join Harry Potter on his way to Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry via the Glenfinnan Viaduct, and set out on Ridley Scott’s Prometheus to discover the origins of humankind – starting the mission at the Quiraing in Skye

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Uisdean and Roddy head out of the kitchen, one to the country and one to the sea, returning with venison sausages for a casserole and a wealth of seafood for a platter

programme.desc: Gudrun and her friend Olaf find a crab and decide to build him a sand castle, but they don't realise how difficult it is for him to hide where there's no water and he is spotted by a keen-eyed, hungry gull

programme.desc: Eilidh Cormack meets young children who are pet owners. Together they show, tell and share how they care for, play and interact with their pets at home

programme.desc: Maurice is very skilled at setting traps, but it's a talent that can cause many problems

programme.desc: Sgallan has a plan – the two bands of robbers to unite, but who will be the leader? Mata or Borca? They will have to fight to establish the winner

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Crumhorn is back and Danger Mouse has released him by mistake

programme.title: An t-Eilean (From Skye with Love)
programme.desc: Preparations are under way for a big dance event in Portree - Strictly Skye. Alasdair, Helen and the team hold the 32nd annual Cancer Research fundraising event in Staffin and Uig postman James has an interesting round

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: The history of the London rush hour, featuring montages of the city's traffic lights and the growth in the usage of cars

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Dreach Ùr
programme.desc: Shona Masson visits a charity shop in Glasgow where she buys a lampshade and a couple of skirts. In Inverness, Angela MacMillan makes them into a mobile for a baby's room

programme.title: Talamh gu Truinnsear
programme.desc: Following gardener Heather Dewar over a year of planting, weeding and cooking. Against the backdrop of the stunning Islay countryside, she shares her horticultural knowledge and favourite recipes. In the second of two programmes, she cooks her favourite dishes using autumn and winter produce

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Personal stories about epilepsy, featuring contributions from some of the 54,000 people in Scotland with the condition, as well as their families and carers. Plus, a look at some of the different types of seizures and how the right kind of treatment can make a significant difference

programme.title: Dan
programme.desc: Poet Peter MacKay talks about poetry and its importance. He discusses how Lewis poet Iain Crichton Smith has influenced him and reads Tha thu air Aigeann M'Inntinn

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Slighe Anndra
programme.desc: John Carmichael visits locations where the Church of Scotland has expatriate congregations. From London to Rotterdam, Geneva and Budapest, he hears worshippers' stories

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Su Pic (Peek Zoo)
programme.desc: There are lots of difficulties at Sù Pìc when the lemurs are being moved to a new enclosure but, fortunately, Dr Sue is available to lend a hand

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop teaches Bing a new game that involves chasing his shadow around the garden

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: When Coco is given a plaster for a cut, Bing decides that he should also get one

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: An unusual monster comes between Twigs and the new badge he is working hard to earn

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: Witzy learns that he should share his toys

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Mischa MacPherson reads Na Bi Draghail, a Chrùbaig Bhig written and illustrated by Chris Haughton and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: Po learns a new Kung Fu technique that leaves his friends with no idea who they are

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: A sumo wrestling competition

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to create ghostly slime and turn drinks cans into disco dancers. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald

programme.title: Kerry is Kirsty
programme.desc: Starting at the foot of Stac Polly, sisters Kerry and Kirsty MacPhee cycle round Coigeach with stunning views of the Summer Isles as a reward for their struggle against high winds. They meet up with Seonaidh Charity, who first got to know his chosen route as he'd often travel the road to visit his now fiancee

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary about the polio vaccine, and how children have been taught the rules of the road

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Taigheadais Dùthchail (Rural Housing)
programme.desc: Ewen MacKinnon looks at the housing that emerged after the Second World War, from social housing and kit houses, to eco friendly modern homes

programme.title: God Save The Quine
programme.desc: Fiona MacKenzie explores the journeys travelled by Scotland's most revered female musicians - Annie Lennox, Barbara Dickson, Sharleen Spiteri, Eddie Reader, Clare Grogan, Lorraine McIntosh, KT Tunstall, Emma Pollock, Amy McDonald, Lauren Mayberry, Be Charlotte and newly emerging talent Kitti

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon escapes city life for the solitude and scenery of the island of Rum

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis presents the music series, offering musicians an opportunity to perform directly from their homes to viewing fans

programme.title: Ever Believe Me - Sgeulachd Madeleine Smith (The Madeleine Smith Story)
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the 19th-century murder case involving Madeleine Smith, asking whether the Glasgow socialite really did poison her lover with arsenic. Presented by John Morrison

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Willy Wonka and the Chocolate Factory (1971)
programme.desc: An eccentric confectionery king announces that five children will be given a tour of his wondrous factory. The winners turn out to be a kind-hearted boy from a penniless family and four obnoxious brats, who face a terrible fate when they misbehave. Musical fantasy, based on Roald Dahl's classic book Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, starring Gene Wilder, Jack Albertson, Peter Ostrum and Roy Kinnear

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Family and friends from Kent answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Stuart, Eve, Paula and Adam pit their wits against one of the Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim spins her web and manipulates the situation. Leyla and Liam's wedding preparations are picking up. Leanna drops some hints to Billy

programme.title: The Masked Dancer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts as five of the remaining 10 disguised celebrities return to the stage with new routines, with Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Mo Gilligan and Oti Mabuse trying to guess who they are. Two of the contestants then compete in a dramatic dance-off, before the identity of the third incognito star is revealed.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jenny faces danger upon learning the truth about Sharon. Nina is devastated by Asha's betrayal. Sarah persuades Adam to back out of a promise

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The River Wild (1994)
programme.desc: A headstrong white-water rafting expert embarks on a holiday with her family, but their fun comes to an abrupt end when two killers show up. The fugitives' only escape route from the law is down a raging river, so they take the woman and her son hostage to act as guides. Meanwhile, her husband desperately tries to keep track from the riverbank, waiting for his opportunity to launch a rescue attempt. Action thriller, starring Meryl Streep, Kevin Bacon, David Strathairn, Joseph Mazzello and John C Reilly

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as Kelly-Anne and Craig from Greenock, Kent couple Paul and Catharine, and husband-and-wife team Daniel and Hind from London use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leave with nothing

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the friendly at Riverside Stadium in Middlesbrough, as the teams continue preparations for Euro 2020. This match and Sunday's clash with Romania will be the final opportunities for the England players to impress Gareth Southgate ahead of their opener against Croatia on June 13, as the nations meet for the first time since 2007, when Peter Crouch scored the only goal of the game in Vienna. With analysis from Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Eni Aluko, and commentary by Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland begins to socialise with Dr Fist, but a well-meaning attempt to get in touch with the medic's old friends reveals a potentially life-threatening secret. Meanwhile, Junior and Rallo open a mobile catering business at school, but their enterprise faces competition

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of a young man's body in an area notorious for illicit teenage gatherings, the corpse wrapped in plastic sheeting and having received a blow to the head. Vera follows the trail to a funfair where the victim used to work and a church where he sought refuge, having recently been made homeless. She delves into his family history to discover why he was on the streets when his parents lived nearby, while forensic evidence implicates the son of the vicar, who has a criminal past

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. Milner finds his loyalty divided when Foyle is placed under house arrest, but the determined investigator vows to prove his innocence. Drama series, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne is worried that Darren is plotting revenge against her and Nick. Vera interviews new barmaids for the Rovers. Audrey warns Spider that she can fight a dirty election

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv attempts to apologise to Kelly. Ollie bunks off school again and ends up in danger. Kelly is not impressed with Tricia's efforts to be a lady

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man begins receiving nuisance calls after a mysterious stranger moves into the village. Meanwhile, Gina's love life hots up in the kitchen - with serious repercussions. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: That's My Boy

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The rent money disappears, forcing Jo to consider selling a prized possession. Flat-share comedy, with Sally Thomsett, Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jo makes the rash assumption that a man has taken violent steps to deal with his truanting son, and Greengrass sets up an illegal stud with a horse-training pal. Meanwhile, PC Ventress starts behaving oddly. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Angie and Sean wait in the hospital for news on Ollie. Tricia is bitter about Bernice's good luck. Sean and Zak scuffle with each other over what happened to Ollie

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice is nervous about the handover of the pub. Graham lets himself into Kathy's cottage to tidy it up for her return. Tricia takes a shine to Richie

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec and Jack want rid of Sandy, but Vera is adamant he stays. Roy sets off to Amsterdam to find Hayley. Greg is alarmed when Maxine suggests he moves in with her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim decides to give up alcohol. Emily buys Spider a suit to help with his campaign. Hayley is happy to see Roy, but neither of them know where they stand

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is discovered on the Northumberland moors, and identified as a woman who made a distressed 999 call on the night of her death - suggesting she may have been abducted by someone she knew. Vera takes on the case, and discovers the victim was a controversial figure in her home town, who had a complicated relationship with her family and a number of well-hidden secrets

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell

programme.title: Rising Damp (1980)
programme.desc: Seedy landlord Rigsby takes in a seemingly respectable new lodger who jeopardises his efforts to romance tenant Miss Jones - but the new arrival may not be all he appears. Meanwhile, the addition of a rugby playing art student to the house sparks a fitness craze that leads Rigsby to enter a boxing match. Comedy based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington

Meanwhile, trustworthy Greengrass becomes a gamekeeper and the truth behind Alf's strange behaviour is revealed. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris finds his tyres slashed. Mandy is released from jail. Kelly demands that Scott leaves her alone. Kathy and Alice receive a surprise visitor

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec tries to sign Sandy up for Golden Years. Roy and Hayley enjoy themselves sightseeing in Amsterdam. Maxine has no luck raising the deposit for the flat

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Hayley is sad that Roy has to return to England. Sandy starts doing some escort work for Alec. Emily organises a campaign photo shoot for Spider

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of a young man's body in an area notorious for illicit teenage gatherings, the corpse wrapped in plastic sheeting and having received a blow to the head. Vera follows the trail to a funfair where the victim used to work and a church where he sought refuge, having recently been made homeless. She delves into his family history to discover why he was on the streets when his parents lived nearby, while forensic evidence implicates the son of the vicar, who has a criminal past

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. Milner finds his loyalty divided when Foyle is placed under house arrest, but the determined investigator vows to prove his innocence. Drama series, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. The murder of a widow soon gives Frost a chance to prove his worth again, but the investigation only reveals an intriguing web of deceit, and it isn't until a forensic psychologist joins the team that they realise a serial killer is at work. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.desc: Claudia tells Zoe that Chris has not come home. Kathy tells Elsa to leave her and Alice alone. Angie hears that Chris's car has been found abandoned on waste ground in Hotten

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is smitten by Lorraine but she is more interested in Spider. Maxine tells Greg she is in love with him. Roy takes Hayley boating and suggests they start an affair

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Stanhope and her team, including new recruit DC Hicham Cherradi, travel to a remote country house when the woman looking after it is killed. No sooner have they arrived, than a second body is discovered. Vera discovers the two victims met the night before their deaths, and goes in search of a connection between them. Forensics evidence reveals that one was definitely present when the other died - but when no murder weapon or motive can be found, Vera begins to suspect a third party was involved

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the second day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, featuring first-round matches from both the men's and women's draws. Rafael Nadal continued his unprecedented dominance of the event last year, getting his tournament under way with a straight-sets victory over Egor Gerasimov and going on to win the title for the 13th time in his career. Another all-time great also progressed on day one, as Serena Williams defeated Kristie Ahn 7-6, 6-0. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Mrs Peel is kidnapped by a mad Teutonic film director, who is attempting to make a film about her life. Unfortunately for Peel it ends with her death, leaving Steed in a hurry to save her. Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg star

programme.desc: A selection of the best shots from ITV Sports' snooker coverage

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the third day of the second Grand Slam of the year, held at Roland Garros, where the men's and women's first-round matches are scheduled to reach a conclusion. Top men's seed Novak Djokovic was in dominant form as he overwhelmed Mikael Ymer 6-0, 6-2, 6-3 at this stage last year, after second women's seed Karolina Pliskova survived a scare against qualifier Mayar Sherif before prevailing 6-7, 6-2, 6-4. Meanwhile, Greek prodigy Stefanos Tsitsipas came back from two sets down to eventually overcome Jaume Munar in one of the day's best matches. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the fourth day of the second Grand Slam of the year, held at Roland Garros, where the men's and women's second-round matches are scheduled to get under way. There were comfortable straight-set victories for Rafael Nadal, Dominic Thiem and Simona Halep against Mackenzie McDonald, Jack Sock and Irina-Camelia Begu respectively at this stage last year. However, there was disappointment for three-time champion Serena Williams, as injury forced her to give a walkover to wild card Tsvetana Pironkova. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Mrs Peel is kidnapped by a mad Teutonic film director, who is attempting to make a film about her life. Unfortunately for Peel it ends with her death, leaving Steed in a hurry to save her. Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg star

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A selection of the best shots from ITV Sports' snooker coverage

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the third day of the second Grand Slam of the year, held at Roland Garros, where the men's and women's first-round matches are scheduled to reach a conclusion. Top men's seed Novak Djokovic was in dominant form as he overwhelmed Mikael Ymer 6-0, 6-2, 6-3 at this stage last year, after second women's seed Karolina Pliskova survived a scare against qualifier Mayar Sherif before prevailing 6-7, 6-2, 6-4. Meanwhile, Greek prodigy Stefanos Tsitsipas came back from two sets down to eventually overcome Jaume Munar in one of the day's best matches. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage three, which featured a 172.5km route from Langeac to Saint-Haon-Le-Vieux. Gary Imlach presents, with commentary by Simon Brotherton and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe

programme.title: World Superbike Highlights

programme.title: The Avengers

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the fourth day of the second Grand Slam of the year, held at Roland Garros, where the men's and women's second-round matches are scheduled to get under way. There were comfortable straight-set victories for Rafael Nadal, Dominic Thiem and Simona Halep against Mackenzie McDonald, Jack Sock and Irina-Camelia Begu respectively at this stage last year. However, there was disappointment for three-time champion Serena Williams, as injury forced her to give a walkover to wild card Tsvetana Pironkova. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Cycling: Criterium du Dauphine Highlights

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is housesitting for Weasel. When he sees him returning from the grocery store with Fizz, he thinks they're burglars!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are at the supermarket buying ingredients to make the world's best cake. They bump into Magoo, who thinks he's at a carnival!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is lost in a pyramid but he is in fact in a big conference room where Fizz is about to perform a stand-up act.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has dressed Weasel up as a supervillain in order to pretend to save the city. Magoo mistakes his disguised neighbour for an actress in danger.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel accidentally launches Fizz next to Magoo. The latter, believing he is driving through a wildlife reserve, mistakes Fizz for a lost child.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's underwater scooting and has discovered Atlantis. In fact, he's in New York, riding Fizz's latest invention.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are trying to steal the president's jokebook. Magoo, thinking he is visiting a museum of sorcery, mistakes Weasel for a wizard.

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has invented a hoverboard. After a slip-up, the board ends up in the hands of Magoo, who thinks he has found a new ironing board.

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: In the desert, Fizz and Weasel plan a fireworks display for Fizz. Mountain Rescuer Magoo mistakes Weasel for a skier in distress.

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo takes Fizz for a toy containing top secret information, which he steals from a secret agent, but it's actually just weasel.

programme.desc: The team presents a special episode devoted to barbecues. Jimmy Doherty looks at the difference between marinades and dry rubs, Andi Oliver discovers the trick to barbecuing halloumi, Kate Quilton investigates the best ways to keep drinks cool, and Briony May Williams heads to Wales to solve the mystery of edible charcoal. Plus, the reason why supermarket fruit salad rarely contains strawberries

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
programme.desc: Prue Leith and Dr Rupy Aujla come to the aid of a busy single mother in West Yorkshire who wants to prepare fresh, nutritious vegan meals for her two daughters, but often ends up resorting to takeaways and frozen food, leaving their food waste out of control. The duo demonstrates how to make BBQ corn ribs with home-made baked beans and slaw, a spring Panzanella salad that uses up one of the UK's most wasted foods, and a novel way to transform flat fizzy pop

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 69-year-old man is rushed to St George's after collapsing on a tennis court, and the resus team has to work quickly to get his dangerously high heart rate under control. Other patients include a father suffering from shortness of breath and a young man who sustained a head injury playing football. Newly qualified consultant Mehrad recounts what it's like to work under pressure and the satisfaction of saving a patient's life

programme.title: The Truth about Police Stop & Search
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas examines the controversial policing policy, giving black men issued with hidden cameras the chance to share their experiences of being stopped and searched. The programme examines the scale of the issue, and its wide-reaching social, psychological and personal consequences for black men, who are nine times more likely to be stopped and searched than white men

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics Paul Johnson and Alex Flashwood rush through traffic, searching for a father and his two-month-old baby, who has stopped breathing

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to transform the fortunes of Sebastian's, a pizzeria in Burbank, Los Angeles, which is failing to attract customers, leaving the owner in debt and struggling to support his family. Gordon is horrified to discover pre-processed and frozen ingredients on the menu, but there seems to be little he can do to help when he is thrown out of the kitchen

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In West Yorkshire, three couples, including Katie and Neil, hope their hosting and cookery is enough to win them the prize money

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Pastry chefs from hotels, restaurants and small businesses in Cardiff, Bristol, Norfolk, Brighton and London compete to be crowned best patisserie team in Britain. The teams face testing challenges judged by two world-class patisserie experts, Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden. For their first challenge, the teams must produce two different types of miniature classics - Tarte Piémontaise and the Jaffa Cake. They must make 24 perfect and identical items of each pastry in just three and a half hours

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Egos inflate to record proportions as the Crane brothers clash during a court battle to decide the mental competence of an eccentric millionaire. Guest starring Donald O'Connor

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink falls head over heels for an policewoman, unaware she finds his dad a much more interesting proposition. Comedy, guest starring Malcolm in the Middle's Jane Kaczmarek

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: The language barrier almost gets Johnny and Ashley into trouble as they go shopping for antiques. In Normandy, new owners Anna and Philipp see if their ancient well can be of use

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, married couple Victoria and Andrew kick off with an Italian-themed menu. This divides girlfriends Ciara and Carley, while Terri and husband David aren't keen on the couples' decision to invite a gospel choir to perform. Day two sees fitness-loving Ciara and Carley cooking up a healthy, clean-eating menu. However, Victoria and Andrew are left feeling worse for wear after a game of smoothie pong involving sprout juice, and Terri is less than pleased with the sportswear dress code. Finally, it's retro-loving Terri and David's chance to bring back the '70s to try to bag the grand

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game where contestants pit their wits against the clock. Janet Street-Porter is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent

programme.title: The Answer Trap
programme.desc: Sisters Morven and Robyn from Surrey take on Steve and Linda, a couple from Market Weighton in East Yorkshire, hoping to reach the £10,000 final. Hosted by Anita Rani

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Diane and Gary, who are looking for an accessible holiday home with a pool in the all-year-round sunshine of Paphos in Cyprus

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Old Orleton Inn in Wellington, Shropshire, where an award-winning breakfast is on offer - but a lack of showers does not go down well

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Culinary contender Natasha Worral hopes her daughter's favourite dish of Greek meatballs will stump chef Sophie Michell to get some money in her prize pot. Natasha also has to beat Sophie at her own family favourite of truffle mac 'n' cheese, as she cooks herself closer to that enticing jackpot sum. Hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Duff beer's mascot Duffman injures himself after one too many pelvic thrusts, so a nationwide reality contest is launched - So You Think You Can Duff - to find his replacement. Homer wins hands down, only to learn that the main job requirement is that he has to stay sober at all times. But what at first sounds like an impossible task soon proves to be an enlightening lifestyle change as Homer realises alcohol isn't necessary to have a good time. With the guest voices of Cat Deeley, Stacy Keach and R Lee Ermey

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Summer does her best to prove to Brody how serious she is about their relationship, and Verity confides in Tony about her fears for Diane's mental health

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles present as the pastry chefs are challenged to make a cube-shaped dessert, with sharp edges from every angle, and magical confections that look exactly like mushrooms but taste anything but - all in just three and a half hours. And that's just their first task. For their second, judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden ask them to create a heroes and villains-themed showpiece adorned with 160 macarons. Which team will rise to the occasion?

programme.title: The Anti-Vax Conspiracy
programme.desc: An investigation into the international anti-vaccination movement and its message that Covid-19 is a myth and part of a government plot to inject people with dangerous vaccines. The documentary goes inside the world of the 'anti-vaxxers', ranging from those profiting from lethal fake cures to Piers Corbyn, brother of the former Labour leader, who has compared the vaccine rollout to experiments in wartime concentration camps

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A look back at the times that the unique dating show has broken new ground, featuring stories from across the spectrum of gender and sexuality. There is also a catch-up with Lauren Harries, celebrated transsexual woman and the first celebrity to appear in a pod, and Josh, who was on the third series, to find out if he is still a virgin

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Thirty-four-year-old plumber Jamie hasn't had a proper girlfriend for almost 10 years. His date, Leigh, is seeking someone muscley and good with his hands. Shelly is a 57-year-old personal trainer who has been single for seven years. Her date is David, a 61-year-old retired firefighter who's been single for four years. Father-of-two Nathan has had a successful career in sales. His date Lisa seeks someone 'nicer' than the guys she usually dates. Last into the restaurant is 27-year-old steampunk enthusiast Joe. His date is 29-year-old Jess, who used to work at the Black Country Living Museum

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of Finn McCool's, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, New York. The chances of the establishment becoming a success are being ruined by the owner Buddy Mazzio's two sons, bartender Jason and chef Brian, who are constantly fighting with each other, prompting Gordon to have a heart-to-heart with the family

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Double-act Cheech and Chong split during their Springfield reunion show, leaving Homer to step into the breach and help out one of his comedy heroes. The new line-up is so successful that Cheech decides to partner Homer full-time, leaving Chong to recruit Principal Skinner as the other half of his new act

programme.title: Eeb Allay Ooo! (2019)
programme.desc: A young migrant in New Delhi, scores the unusual government job of keeping monkeys away from public buildings, and focuses on his ensuing struggles and disillusionment with the work. Starring Shardul Bharadwaj, Mahender Nath, Nutan Sinha, Shashi Bhushan and Naina Sareen

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a primary school teacher in Southend hoping to build a classroom inside a salvaged plane, and in Devon he finds out about a cricket-mad couple's plans to turn an old scorebox into a garden hangout. programme.desc: Part one of three. When councilwoman Janet Eldridge visits the bar during her re-election campaign, Sam is smitten, so a jealous Diane joins the opposition to save him from the politician's dubious charms. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Shelley Long

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A discovery makes Ray worry about his mortality - and prompts him to compile a list of things he wants to do before he dies. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.desc: Debra clears out all the kids' old baby clothes and gives them to a jumble sale, making Ray realise she is resigned to not having any more children

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of two. Flashback episode in which the Barones recall their courtship and wedding, beginning with Ray's nervous marriage proposal - and subsequent uncertainty over Debra's motive for accepting

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Catching up with two sets of house-hunters with different requirements. In 2010, Carole and Alan, and their daughter Lauren, were casting a wide net over Somerset and Devon. They needed a rural spot to accommodate Lauren's dog training business, as well as a home with the potential to do holiday lets to keep Carole and Alan busy. And in 2019, Ros and Hannah wanted to buy their first home together, but finding somewhere that ticked all of their boxes proved. Now they programme returns to see how both couples are getting on

programme.title: Before We Die
programme.desc: Hannah and her new partner, Billy Murdoch, start investigating the Mimica family, believing them to be responsible for Sean's murder. They discover that the clan are planning to smuggle a massive quantity of cocaine into the country. But when Hannah learns the identity of Sean's contact, she immediately wants to pull the plug on the investigation. Crime thriller, starring Lesley Sharp and Vincent Regan

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits a gastropub in Oak Park, California, pitched close to ranches often used in TV and film productions. Kirin and Angelika Stone own the place, but have lost their home in the wildfires that ravaged California. Can Gordon and his team help save their restaurant?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 69-year-old man is rushed to St George's after collapsing on a tennis court, and the resus team has to work quickly to get his dangerously high heart rate under control. Other patients include a father suffering from shortness of breath and a young man who sustained a head injury playing football. Newly qualified consultant Mehrad recounts what it's like to work under pressure and the satisfaction of saving a patient's life

programme.title: 21 Day Body Turnaround with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The journalist and doctor asks five volunteers to put their lives in his hands as he attempts to turn their health around in just 21 days. Initial testing reveals all of them to likely be at risk from debilitating conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, heart disease, chronic inflammation and stress. Most have the aerobic fitness age of someone at least 20 years older. In the first episode, Michael investigates their digestive health and starts them out on a new training plan in conjunction with GP and fitness trainer Dr Folusha Oluwajana

programme.title: The Pyramid (2014)
programme.desc: A previously unexplored pyramid is discovered in the Egyptian desert, and a party of archaeologists sets out to uncover its secrets. The explorers soon find the tomb is riddled with deadly traps, but this proves the least of their worries when they realise it was built to contain dark forces that they have now unleashed. Horror, starring Ashley Hinshaw and Denis O'Hare

programme.desc: George Clarke meets a couple who are planning to renovate a derelict 18th-century hunting lodge in Warwickshire. Richard Keeble and Jane Kennard have spent £100,000 on structural repairs and earmarked the same sum for the interior. programme.desc: Highlights of Jane's cruise from Miami, around the Caribbean to Puerto Rico

Sci-fi drama, starring Charlton Heston, Roddy McDowall, Kim Hunter and Maurice Evans

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Acting on intelligence, the eight members of Derbyshire's Road Crime Team set out to intercept a car suspected of being used in drug deals, and detain three men, including the main target they were looking for. Ant Horsley gives chase when a suspicious car fails to stop late at night, with the vehicle eventually crashing after taking a corner too fast

programme.title: Mrs Thatcher vs the Miners
programme.desc: The behind-the-scenes story of how Margaret Thatcher defeated the miners in 1984/85 and changed the face of Britain forever, featuring contributions from Downing Street insiders and those involved in the bloody pitched battles between those on strike and those who wanted to work. The documentary also examines the critical role of her secret weapon, David Hart, an Eton-educated avant-garde filmmaker, an 'irregular' who worked with Thatcher behind the scenes. He was her enforcer and ultimately delivered the final blow

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: When the red phone rings in the A&E department of Barnsley Hospital, staff rush to help a woman who has arrived in an ambulance and is struggling to breathe. Former showbiz pianist Terry (84), comes in with a badly broken ankle and the fear he may never dance again

programme.title: Hoarders
programme.desc: Documentary following people with a compulsion to hoard. The first episode features a woman diagnosed with cancer whose home is full of rotten food and dead mice, a father whose relationship with his daughter has suffered as a result of his obsession, and a Huddersfield man whose clutter caught fire, burning his house to the ground

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth's 7 Year Itch
programme.desc: The second of two programmes in which Ruth Langsford and Eamonn Holmes, having celebrated their seventh wedding anniversary, meet various other couples to find out how to keep their marriage alive. They include a family where the husband waited seven years to announce out of the blue he wanted to be a woman, and a couple who use fantasy role play to escape the pressure of raising two autistic children. They also explore meet a pair of swingers and two Indian childhood sweethearts who defied the custom of arranged marriage

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow examines the life and legacy of German painter Albrecht Durer, who set a new benchmark in self-portraiture and raised the artistic reputation of northern Europe to a level rivaling that of Renaissance Italy. Like his contemporary Leonardo da Vinci, Durer perceived the world with a scientist's eye, and his detailed studies of plants and animals remain unrivalled to this day

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow examines the work of Italian Renaissance painter Raffaello Santi, known better simply as Raphael, who was born in 1483 and achieved so much in his 37-year life that he was considered equal to Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo. He was highly regarded for his many studies of the Madonna and Child, and artists adopted his idealised forms as the model of beauty until the end of the 19th century

Starring Richard Burton, Richard Todd, Kenneth More, Sean Connery, John Wayne, Robert Mitchum, Henry Fonda, Curt Jurgens, Roddy McDowall and Rod Steiger

programme.title: Hotel for Dogs (2009)
programme.desc: An orphaned brother and sister face losing their beloved dog when they go into a foster home that does not allow pets. The kids find the ideal hiding place in an abandoned hotel, and set about transforming it into luxury canine accommodation for not just their beloved pet, but every stray in town. Family comedy, starring Emma Roberts, Jake T Austin, Don Cheadle and Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee II (1988)
programme.desc: The rugged Australian adventurer sets out to save his reporter girlfriend from New York drug dealers. Having rescued her from their clutches, he realises they will soon be coming after him - so he decides to even the odds by luring them to the Outback for a final showdown. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Paul Hogan, Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Charles S Dutton

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen (2003)
programme.desc: Fearless hunter Allan Quatermain teams up with a host of other characters from Victorian literature - including Captain Nemo, the Invisible Man, Tom Sawyer and Dr Jekyll - to put a stop to the nefarious Fantom's evil plot to bring about a world war. Fantasy adventure, based on Alan Moore's comic-book series, starring Sean Connery in his final screen role, Shane West, Stuart Townsend, Richard Roxburgh, Peta Wilson and Tony Curran

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark (1981)
programme.desc: Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones goes in search of the legendary Ark of the Covenant, a biblical artefact of devastating power. His quest leads him to Egypt, where the Nazis are plotting to use the Ark to further Hitler's plans for world domination - aided by Jones's deceitful French rival. Steven Spielberg's action adventure, with Harrison Ford, Karen Allen, Paul Freeman and John Rhys-Davies

programme.title: Professor Marston and the Wonder Women (2017)
programme.desc: The story of psychologist William Moulton Marston, and his relationship with his wife and their mistress who would inspire the creation of Wonder Woman. Biopic, starring Luke Evans, Rebecca Hall and Bella Heathcote

programme.title: Daphne (2017)
programme.desc: A hedonistic London thirtysomething chef has her life thrown off balance when she witnesses a violent crime. British drama, starring Emily Beecham, Geraldine James and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.title: Night Passage (1957)
programme.desc: The black sheep of a family falls in with a gang of outlaws plotting to rob a train operated by his strait-laced brother, but reckons without his sibling's determination to track him down and set him back on the straight and narrow. Western, starring James Stewart, Audie Murphy, Dan Duryea and Brandon de Wilde

programme.title: Dragoon Wells Massacre (1957)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer is assigned to escort a captured murderer to prison. However, the prisoner and his guard are compelled to join forces to survive when Apache warriors raid their wagon train as it moves across Arizona into hostile territory. Western, starring Barry Sullivan, Dennis O'Keefe, Mona Freeman and Katy Jurado

programme.title: The Weaker Sex (1948)
programme.desc: A housewife regrets not doing more to help the war effort after the ship her son is serving on is damaged in the D-Day Landings. Drama, starring Ursula Jeans and Thora Hird

programme.title: Distant Drums (1951)
programme.desc: An army officer mounts a daring raid through the treacherous Florida swamplands. His mission is to take on a group of marauding Seminole rebels, as well as the gun runners who have been supplying them with weapons, and rescue a group of settlers who have been taken hostage by the tribe. Western, starring Gary Cooper, Mari Aldon, Richard Webb, Ray Teal and Arthur Hunnicutt

programme.title: Mother's Day (2016)
programme.desc: As Mother's Day approaches, an array of seemingly unconnected characters all find themselves reconsidering their relationships with their mothers and what motherhood mean to them. Garry Marshall's ensemble comedy in which various families go through dramatic turning points, starring Julia Roberts, Jennifer Aniston, Kate Hudson, Jason Sudeikis, Jennifer Garner and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: The Darkest Minds (2018)
programme.desc: When a mysterious pandemic wipes out most of America's children, those who survive are gifted with superpowers. However, when they are imprisoned by adults who fear everyone under 18, a group of teens form a resistance group to fight back and reclaim control of their future. Dystopian thriller, starring Amandla Stenberg and Mandy Moore

programme.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral (1994)
programme.desc: A London man's misfortunes in love look set to change when he meets an American beauty. However, the prospect of a transatlantic relationship succeeding seems slim, so the pair go their separate ways - only for him to realise he is unable to get her out of his mind. Richard Curtis's comedy, with Hugh Grant, Andie MacDowell, John Hannah, Charlotte Coleman, Kristin Scott Thomas, Simon Callow and James Fleet

programme.title: Birds of Passage (2018)
programme.desc: In Colombia, Rapayet and his indigenous family get involved in a war to control the business that ends up destroying their lives and culture. Drama, starring Jose Acosta and Natalia Reyes

programme.title: A Night to Remember (1958)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama recreating the sinking of the Titanic, told from the point of view of the ship's second officer as he struggles to get as many passengers and crew off the vessel before it sinks. Meanwhile, the captain of a nearby vessel embarks on a doomed rescue mission. Starring Kenneth More, Honor Blackman, Ronald Allen, Tucker McGuire and David McCallum

programme.title: In Harm's Way (1965)
programme.desc: Two Navy officers are disgraced in the aftermath of the attack on Pearl Harbour, one for a disastrous engagement with the enemy and the other for a drunken brawl after learning of his wife's death. They get another chance to prove their worth when they are placed in charge of a vital counter-offensive. Second World War drama, starring John Wayne, Kirk Douglas and Henry Fonda

programme.title: At Gunpoint (1955)
programme.desc: A peaceable shopkeeper becomes a local hero when he accidentally kills the leader of a gang of bank robbers. However, when the dead bandit's partners in crime come looking for retribution, he finds the friends and neighbours who praised his bravery are now about to abandon him. Western, starring Fred MacMurray, Dorothy Malone and Walter Brennan

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul

programme.title: 12 Years a Slave (2013)
programme.desc: A free black man living in pre-Civil War New York is abducted and sold into slavery. He spends the next 12 years struggling to survive and maintain his dignity in the face of brutal treatment, while clinging to a desperate hope that he can return to his family. Oscar-winning historical drama based on Solomon Northup's autobiographical book, starring Chiwetel Ejiofor, Michael Fassbender, Benedict Cumberbatch, Lupita Nyong'o and Brad Pitt

Ridley Scott's crime thriller, starring Michael Fassbender, Penelope Cruz, Cameron Diaz, Javier Bardem and Brad Pitt

programme.desc: The fourth and final visit is to Tall John's House, Robin and Liza Watson's grand Georgian mansion in the Welsh countryside, where the guests are wowed by the historic building and rooms. Raouf, Chrissie, Clarkey and Katie dig deep to find fault but can only find small problems. Following an afternoon in the billiard room, the guests gush over their friendship at dinner but, when it comes to the question of future meet-ups, Vicky drops a bombshell

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: White Hart Hotel management team Clarkey and Katie raise the issue of paying for their car park as the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers search for heirs on a huge family tree, and spend days trying to track them down. Plus, the investigation into a former civil servant's £160,000 estate, and details of more unclaimed properties

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Elderly cheetah Matrah is ostracised by his brothers Burba and Singha when he starts to show signs of a leg injury. Cameras also follow Chester Zoo's Sumatran orangutans, including adolescent female Tuti and four-month-old Kesuma. And there's also a look at Asian pond turtle Shrek, whose love life proved fatal for a previous mate

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: New owners Amy and Marc have big dreams to turn their 20-room chateau into a B&B and yoga retreat. They first need to replace their defunct sewage system and create a pipe network within the chateau's ancient walls. Meanwhile, musicians Ben and Billie start renovating their chateau's final untouched room - the old library - and restore the skylight complete with its original Victorian mechanism

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Stuart and Rosie Treasurer from the Wirral plan to transform a small bungalow from a 1960s dormer into a slick piece of 21st-century architecture. The couple decapitate the building and balance a new floating timber box on top to create five bedrooms, spending as little as possible on plumbing, electrics and insulation, but stress levels spiral when neighbours grumble at the ultra-modern creation

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes
programme.desc: Exploring some of Britain's most beautiful areas, beginning with the Lake District. The journey takes in the ancient woodlands of Borrowdale and the peak of Helvellyn

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. From a lighthouse cottage on a private island near the Isle of Skye and a stunning tree house in the Wye Valley, to the only nature reserve in England in which you can stay, every episode visits a different region and promises hidden away places offering glorious sanctuary. In the opening edition, Sandi is joined by legendary actor Alison Steadman to seek out extraordinary escapes in the wilds of Suffolk

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Moped delivery man Hussnain, 24, is rushed to A&E after being caught up in an accident involving a police car chasing another vehicle. Seventy-six-year-old Edward is brought in due to his breathing difficulties and a serious pre-existing heart condition, while part-time wrestlers Kieran and James come in after Kieran injures his knee in a bout

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz as Sean Lock and Henning Wehn compete against Jon Richardson and Victoria Coren Mitchell. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters with 'help' from Joe Wilkinson

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes
programme.desc: Exploring some of Britain's most beautiful areas, beginning with the Lake District. The journey takes in the ancient woodlands of Borrowdale and the peak of Helvellyn

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig

In the opening edition, Sandi is joined by legendary actor Alison Steadman to seek out extraordinary escapes in the wilds of Suffolk

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt heads to Italy to find out how supermarkets can sell pesto at an affordable price when the traditional ingredients are expensive, while Jimmy Doherty investigates why so many cockles in UK supermarkets come from abroad when he always thought of them as a quintessentially British staple. Kate Quilton takes a trip around Europe's largest marshmallow factory to find out why the sticky treat goes crispy and melty at the same time over the campfire

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how to create handmade and personalised gifts. She learns about sugarcraft and perfume-making, and also receives a lesson in the art of designing Victorian-style silhouettes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Guy and Julie Sadler from Brighton have travelled all around the world together and have long dreamed of relocating somewhere warm. Now, although they've never been to Spain, they've decided that Almería has the perfect balance of sunshine and affordable property. Laura Hamilton shows them five homes for their £100,000 budget

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 1970s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Wales, where Henry restores a Royal Enfield motorbike, converting it into a classic cafe racer. Simon's choice of an old school house radiator springs a leak, but he gets an 1980s pinball machine back to its best

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A firm traces heirs to the £500,000 estate of a burlesque dancer who performed at Soho's Windmill club throughout the Second World War, while another case uncovers a family's links to a comedy star's radio career. Plus, details of more unclaimed estates

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A young couple need to diversify their French gite business, there is a rocky start for a paragliding company in Spain and two novice winemakers take on the French at a wine fair

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Hillcroft in Torquay, where Martyn and Elaine Flint hope their lovingly decorated, adults-only B&B will prove a hit with their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to the Bear and Swan in Chew Magna in Somerset, where Sam Wilkins and Henry Russell hope to impress with the five-star rooms above their country pub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Guesthouse East in Eastbourne, where Tony and Lisa Partridge hope to impress their guests with their family friendly B&B

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jorge Mesquita plays host at the Shirley B&B, Bournemouth, where he believes the guests will be impressed by his power showers and hopes a go-karting contest will help him win

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jorge Mesquita of the Shirley B&B is taken ill, so the remaining B&Bers fight it out for the prized plaque as they meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A company tries to find beneficiaries to the £400,000 estate of a woman from Enfield, before hearing news that threatens the whole investigation. Elsewhere, the case of a missing man who lived in a shed for 20 years, who left behind a full military pension and substantial savings. Plus, details of unclaimed estates where heirs are still to be found

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The keepers introduce some new, young males to Bertha, the South American blue poison dart frog that loves to wrestle, in the hope that one of them will be strong enough to win her over. A new mandrill monkey named Kamau arrives at the zoo to be introduced to the group, but the keepers are worried that his lack of experience with females might be a problem

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Amy and Marc create an eco-friendly sewage system by connecting the chateau's plumbing to two natural waste ponds 100 metres away. Ben and Billie go fossil hunting in their grounds

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Somerset to see Ed and Vicky Versluys and their cowshed conversion. A year after his last visit and with the house finally complete, Kevin finds out what it's like living in an off-grid home designed for animals

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend travel to South Yorkshire to pick up first for the show - a 1953 Ferguson TE20 tractor. Arable farmer Andrew's family have owned the tractor since it was new and although it hasn't seen action for decades, it's still kept just yards away from his front door. However, it's going to be no easy task to take it away to work on

programme.title: The Lost Lotus: Restoring a Race Car
programme.desc: Philip Glenister and Ant Anstead restore a 1950s Lotus Elite that was designed and built by Formula 1 engineer Colin Chapman as a prototype for the production models that followed. They plan to take it on to the track at Silverstone for the biggest classic car event of the year - even though this risks the vehicle being damaged before it can be sold at auction

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz as Sean Lock and Roisin Conaty compete against Jon Richardson and Nish Kumar. Adam Buxton joins the show's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Rich, 35, is airlifted to the hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision on the M25, and doctors are concerned when they discover he has lost feeling below his chest and can't move his legs. Meanwhile, 36-year-old Justin has had a life-threatening accident while playing football and he is put into an induced coma while a CT scan is carried out, which reveals a potentially life-threatening bleed on his brain

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
